behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: if a template contains any phase-specific instruction text copied from SKILL.md, the lint rule must fail. The failure boundary is local rule logic (string/pattern/AST validation) with no required module handoff, OS, network, subprocess, or model call. Rejection gate: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified by the behavior text "static prompt-template lint check" and "copied ... in the prompt," which describes static analysis rather than runtime infrastructure or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function on template fixtures (clean vs leaked phase text). Assert leaked fixtures are rejected with precise diagnostics and clean fixtures pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: after real harness interpolation/fixture assembly, the final prompt must not contain phase-instruction leakage. The failure appears at module boundaries (template loader + interpolator + fixture merger), not a single pure function. No real network/subprocess required; local wiring is sufficient. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text focuses on "fully assembled prompt ... after interpolation and fixtures" (in-process assembly), not OS/network primitives. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model-output correctness is referenced.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction pipeline with representative fixtures and assert the emitted final prompt string has no leaked phase-instruction segments.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: changing plugin/skill file content changes what context is loaded/used by invocation, without relying on hard-coded prompt rules. This is a module-handoff + local filesystem integration boundary (loader, resolver, invocation builder). Rejection gate: Agentic is not required by this behavior text because it targets source-of-truth wiring ("loads skill context from plugin/skill files") rather than model output quality; System is disqualified since local file reads are enough and no OS/network/subprocess failure mode is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation setup against temporary plugin/skill files, mutate file content, and assert the loaded context in invocation artifacts changes accordingly while hard-coded prompt rules remain absent.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: with identical test prompt text, switching SKILL.md variant (good/degraded/missing) is supported by harness configuration/execution path. Failure is in harness orchestration across prompt input + skill-source selection modules, typically via local filesystem fixtures. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because no real subprocess/network condition is required; Agentic is disqualified because this behavior is runability/configuration, not outcome quality from model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness runs over skill variants using the same prompt literal; assert runs start successfully and the prompt text artifact is byte-identical across variants.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: under identical user prompt, degrading/removing the relevant SKILL.md section causes model behavior to violate the expected structural/behavioral contract and the test fails. Correctness depends on real model decisions from changed context; a stubbed response could hide this failure. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text "behavioral test fails" due to SKILL.md degradation under same prompt, which directly tests model-output effect of context changes. Workflow is not required because this can be exposed in a single model decision path, not necessarily multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test twice (baseline skill vs degraded/missing section) with identical user prompt; assert baseline passes and degraded variant fails on the same assertions.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: capture step executes first and persists raw response before parser/extractor mutation or failure. The failure boundary is ordering between response-capture module and parse/extract module, plus local artifact write. Rejection gate: Agentic is disqualified because model content quality is irrelevant; a deterministic fixture can still catch ordering bugs. System is disqualified because no real network/OS primitive beyond normal local file write is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real harness capture+parse pipeline with a controlled raw response input; induce parse failure and assert raw artifact exists with original content and timestamp/order proving pre-parse capture.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: output containing required keywords but violating schema path/role field placement must fail assertions. This is assertion-logic correctness within one local validator boundary, no I/O required. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets assertion semantics themselves ("Assertions enforce structural ... not only keyword"), which can be validated with crafted local fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion functions with synthetic outputs: keyword-only-but-structurally-invalid samples must fail; structurally valid samples must pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: with a real model run, at least one response passes keyword checks yet fails structural constraints, and structural assertions detect it. This depends on authentic model output variability/content; mocks can mask this gap. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by explicit text "Under live model execution" and by the need to expose regressions from real model behavior. Workflow is not required unless multiple model decisions are chained; this behavior describes a single execution/output check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live-model test cases and evaluate both checkers; assert existence of cases where keyword-only passes but structural assertions fail, and the test suite fails accordingly.
